body:
## What

Adds per-branch preview deployments via the **Sepo preview server** (`preview-api.sepo.sh`) — the same pattern just shipped to `augmented-mind/augmented-mind.github.io`.

A standalone `.github/workflows/preview.yml` builds the site (`npm ci && npm run build` → `public/`), uploads it as an artifact, and deploys a preview through the server using **GitHub Actions OIDC** — **no secrets in the repo**. Tears down on PR close.

**Triggering:** auto-previews every **agent PR** (gated on the `agent/` branch prefix — present at PR open, no label needed). Label overrides: `sepo-preview` opts a human PR in, `no-preview` opts an agent PR out.

## Notes
- Requires the Sepo GitHub App on the repo — org-wide on `self-evolving`, so already covered.
- As this is the literature **template**, repos generated from it inherit the preview workflow.
- Deploy + teardown chain proven end-to-end on augmented-mind #61.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 76 | PARTIAL | 11 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep privileged workflows on trusted runtime | partial | 3/8 | The workflow grants `id-token: write` at job/workflow scope, then runs PR-controlled `npm ci` / `npm run build` before the OIDC deploy step in the same job: [preview.yml#L5-L30](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L5-L30), [#L43-L48](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L43-L48). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | partial | 3/7 | Preview comments use `gh pr comment --edit-last` without a stable marker or lookup, so reruns can edit the token actor’s unrelated last comment: [preview.yml#L54-L62](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L54-L62). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 5/7 | README documents the policy and public-preview risk, but says `sepo-preview` previews “any other pull request” while the workflow rejects fork PRs: [README#L186-L190](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/README.md#L186-L190), [preview.yml#L17](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L17). |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | README names the variable, explains defaults, warns private repos, and gives the exact `gh variable set` command: [README#L172-L184](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/README.md#L172-L184). |
| communication / generic | Audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The new section starts with what PR previews do, then moves into policy and operational details: [README#L168-L192](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/README.md#L168-L192). |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | The posted PR comment is concise: `Sepo preview: $URL`: [preview.yml#L60-L62](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L60-L62). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` is capability-oriented and supports `auto`/`all`/`off`: [preview.yml#L11-L16](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L11-L16). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Defaults are minimal: public repos auto-preview, private repos off, labels override per PR: [preview.yml#L14-L21](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L14-L21). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Uses standard Actions primitives and existing repo build command rather than adding a custom deployment subsystem: [preview.yml#L24-L35](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L24-L35). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Bound transient retries | partial | 3/7 | External OIDC/server calls use plain `curl` with no bounded retry/backoff for transient 5xx, timeout, or connection failures: [preview.yml#L43-L50](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L43-L50), [#L76-L82](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L76-L82). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Scope is limited to the preview workflow and matching README section. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric miss is privilege isolation. GitHub’s OIDC docs state that a job with `id-token: write` can request a JWT via runner-provided OIDC request variables, so the build should run in a non-OIDC job and the deploy/comment step should run in a separate privileged job with no PR-controlled scripts.
- Current checks show the preview workflow skipped on this private PR under the default policy, which matches the documented private-repo default.
- Smallest useful follow-up: split build/upload from OIDC deploy, replace `--edit-last` with a stable marker/update path, and document the same-repo-only preview restriction.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Privileged OIDC deploy permissions are available in the same job that runs PR-controlled build scripts. This partially violates the trusted-runtime rubric and should be isolated into separate jobs.
- **WARNING:** Preview comment updates are not stable-idempotent because `--edit-last` can target an unrelated last comment by the same token actor.
- **INFO:** External preview API calls should use bounded retries for transient failures while keeping validation/auth failures terminal.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #11 adds a Sepo preview deployment workflow for pull requests. The workflow builds Quartz output, uploads `public/` as an artifact, and asks `preview-api.sepo.sh` to deploy it using GitHub Actions OIDC instead of repository secrets. It also documents `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY`, private-repo defaults, label overrides, and teardown-on-close behavior in the README. The main review question is whether this template should ship a PR-triggered preview workflow with privileged deploy/comment capabilities.

## Review
The PR is close in shape, but it still has one blocking trust-boundary issue in the workflow design. Reviewers disagreed on severity: Claude marked it shippable, rubrics marked privilege isolation as a warning, and Codex marked it blocking; this synthesis treats it as blocking because the workflow runs PR-controlled build code in the same job that can mint the Sepo OIDC token.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Privileged preview deploy runs in a PR-controlled build job | BLOCKING | [`preview.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L5) grants `id-token: write` and PR-write at workflow scope, then checks out and builds PR-controlled code before requesting OIDC. Split the untrusted build from the trusted deploy/comment path before merge. |
| Preview comments are not updated by stable identity | WARNING | [`gh pr comment --edit-last`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/0bf365c78ca0339169a11b0d60b134c24937e336/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L61) edits the last comment by the current token actor, not necessarily the Sepo preview comment. Use a hidden marker or stored comment id. |
| Preview lifecycle/docs leave label behavior ambiguous | WARNING | The workflow only tears down on PR close and does not handle `no-preview` added after deployment or `sepo-preview` removed after opt-in. Either implement those label transitions or document that labels only affect future deploys. |
| Preview API calls lack bounded transient retries | INFO | The deploy and teardown `curl` calls fail immediately on transient network or 5xx failures. This is optional hardening, not a merge blocker. |

## Progress
- Current PR discussion already includes a rubrics review covering the OIDC/job-scope and `--edit-last` concerns, so I did not duplicate those as inline comments.
- Posted one new inline comment for the teardown/label-transition mismatch: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/11#discussion_r3354058219
- Existing inline comments and review threads were empty before posting; no threads were resolved or minimized.
- Current preview jobs are skipped on this private template under the default policy, matching the README’s private-repo default.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Privileged preview deploy runs in a PR-controlled build job</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow-level permissions include `id-token: write` and `pull-requests: write`, while the `deploy` job runs `actions/checkout`, `npm ci`, and `npm run build` before the OIDC deploy step. GitHub documents that `id-token: write` allows JWT requests and that `pull_request` workflows run against PR merge content.

**Candidate solutions:** Use a read-only PR build/upload job, then a trusted default-branch deploy/comment path that does not check out or execute PR code. Revalidate PR number, head SHA, same-repo status, labels, policy, repository visibility, and artifact provenance before minting OIDC or commenting.

**Comments:** This is the key reviewer disagreement: one review marked it shippable, but the current diff still supports the trust-boundary concern.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview comments are not updated by stable identity</summary>

**Cause:** `gh pr comment --edit-last` targets the last comment by the current user. In Actions, that can be an unrelated `github-actions[bot]` comment from another workflow.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a hidden marker such as `<!-- sepo-preview-comment -->`, search existing PR comments for that marker, update the matching comment by id, and create one if absent.

**Comments:** Codex and rubrics both flagged this; Claude treated the fallback as acceptable.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview lifecycle/docs leave label behavior ambiguous</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow listens for `labeled` but only deploys on `sepo-preview`; it does not listen for `unlabeled`, and teardown only runs on `closed`. The README says `sepo-preview` previews other PRs and `no-preview` skips a PR, which can imply live label changes retract or redeploy previews.

**Candidate solutions:** Handle `labeled` with `no-preview` and `unlabeled` for `sepo-preview`, or explicitly document that label changes only affect future deploys and closing the PR is the teardown path. Also clarify that fork PRs are excluded by the same-repo guard.

**Comments:** I posted one inline comment for this because it was not already covered in the live PR discussion.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview API calls lack bounded transient retries</summary>

**Cause:** The OIDC, deploy, and teardown calls use plain `curl` without retry, timeout, or transient 5xx handling.

**Candidate solutions:** Add bounded retry/timeout flags for transient failures while keeping auth, validation, and expected 4xx failures terminal.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: the blocking trust-boundary issue is concrete, localized to `.github/workflows/preview.yml`, and safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Split preview build/upload from the trusted OIDC deploy/comment path so PR-controlled scripts run without `id-token: write` or PR-write permissions.
- [ ] Replace `gh pr comment --edit-last` with marker/comment-id based preview comment updates.
- [ ] Clarify or implement live label-transition behavior for `sepo-preview` and `no-preview`.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 94 | PARTIAL | 12 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep privileged workflows on trusted runtime | pass | 8/8 | The build job runs PR code without OIDC/PR-write, while deploy has `id-token: write` and `pull-requests: write` but no checkout/build step: [preview.yml#L25-L52](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L25-L52). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | Preview comments use a stable hidden marker and update the matching comment id before creating a new one: [preview.yml#L82-L90](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L82-L90). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents same-repo-only previews, fork exclusion, private defaults, and future-only label/policy effects: [README#L172-L192](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/README.md#L172-L192). |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | The docs give the exact repo variable and `gh variable set` command for private opt-in: [README#L180-L184](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/README.md#L180-L184). |
| communication / generic | Audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The section starts with what preview deployments do, then explains policy, opt-in, labels, and teardown: [README#L168-L192](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/README.md#L168-L192). |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | The PR comment body is concise and user-facing: marker plus `Sepo preview: $URL`: [preview.yml#L82-L83](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L82-L83). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` names the capability and covers `auto`/`all`/`off`: [README#L172-L179](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/README.md#L172-L179). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Defaults keep private repos off, public repos automatic, and labels provide narrow per-PR override/opt-out behavior: [preview.yml#L13-L24](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L13-L24). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Uses standard Actions primitives and the existing `npm ci` / `npm run build` site build rather than adding a custom subsystem: [preview.yml#L31-L45](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L31-L45). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Bound transient retries | partial | 4/7 | OIDC token fetches have bounded retries/timeouts, but preview-server deploy/teardown POSTs only set timeouts and do not retry transient 5xx/rate-limit/connection failures: [preview.yml#L61-L68](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L61-L68), [preview.yml#L105-L111](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L105-L111). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 5/7 | The enum-like policy is documented, but malformed values implicitly behave like `auto` rather than failing or warning clearly: [preview.yml#L13-L20](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L13-L20). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Scope is limited to the new preview workflow and matching README documentation. |

## Notes

- The previous privilege and idempotency concerns are addressed in the current head: deploy is separated from PR build code, and comments are marker/id based.
- Remaining misses are hardening-level: add bounded retries around preview API POSTs, and consider rejecting or warning on invalid `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` values.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Preview API deploy/teardown calls still lack bounded retries for transient failures.
- **INFO:** `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` accepts undocumented values by falling through to auto-like behavior.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #11 adds `.github/workflows/preview.yml` to publish per-branch Sepo preview deployments for qualifying pull requests. The workflow builds the Quartz site, uploads `public/` as an artifact, and calls `preview-api.sepo.sh` with GitHub Actions OIDC, avoiding repository deploy secrets. It also documents `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY`, same-repo and fork behavior, public-preview risk, label overrides, and teardown on close. The current head `c5908eb` is a revision after earlier review feedback.

## Review
The updated PR fixes the original single-job OIDC exposure and unstable `--edit-last` preview comment update, but one trust-boundary issue remains. Reviewers disagree on the severity of the split-job design; this synthesis treats it as blocking because the privileged deploy job is still defined by `pull_request` workflow code from the unmerged PR.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Privileged deploy is still PR-workflow-defined | BLOCKING | The deploy job has `id-token: write` and `pull-requests: write` at [`.github/workflows/preview.yml#L46`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L46), but the job shell is still supplied by the PR’s `pull_request` workflow. A future PR can modify that privileged job unless deploy/comment/teardown move to trusted default-branch workflow code. |
| Teardown can miss opt-in previews after label removal | WARNING | The teardown guard at [`.github/workflows/preview.yml#L92`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/c5908eb7b6093de23b1e0be3e5bc6991ad44f6c3/.github/workflows/preview.yml#L92) only runs for agent branches or PRs that still have `sepo-preview` when closed. A same-repo human PR can deploy with the label, remove it, then close without teardown. |
| Preview hardening remains optional | INFO | Rubrics noted that preview-server POSTs lack bounded transient retries and invalid `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` values fall through to auto-like behavior. These are hardening items, not blockers. |

## Progress
- Fixed from the prior synthesis: PR-controlled `npm ci` / `npm run build` now run in a separate `build` job without OIDC or PR-write permissions.
- Fixed from the prior synthesis: preview comments now use a hidden marker and update by comment id instead of `gh pr comment --edit-last`.
- README now documents same-repo-only previews, fork exclusion, private-repo defaults, and future-only label/policy effects.
- Posted one new inline comment for the remaining privileged-runtime issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/11#discussion_r3357532405
- Existing line 92 inline feedback already covers the label-removal teardown risk; no threads were resolved, minimized, or replied to.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Privileged deploy is still PR-workflow-defined</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow now separates build and deploy jobs, but the privileged deploy/comment shell is still part of the PR’s `pull_request` workflow file. The current PR itself produced a `pull_request` Preview run at head `c5908eb`, showing this workflow definition is sourced from the PR head before merge.

**Candidate solutions:** Move deploy/comment/teardown into trusted default-branch workflow code, such as a `workflow_run` or carefully constrained `pull_request_target` workflow that never checks out or executes PR code. Keep the PR build unprivileged, then revalidate PR number, head SHA, same-repo status, labels, policy, and artifact provenance before minting OIDC or writing comments.

**Comments:** Claude and rubrics considered the split-job structure sufficient; Codex flagged the remaining workflow-definition trust boundary. The current diff supports the blocker.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Teardown can miss opt-in previews after label removal</summary>

**Cause:** Teardown requires either an `agent/` branch or the current `sepo-preview` label on close. If a same-repo human PR deployed while labeled and the label is later removed, the README says closing the PR tears down the preview, but this job will not run.

**Candidate solutions:** Run teardown for every closed same-repo PR, since the endpoint already accepts `404`, or persist/derive preview existence independently of the current label set.

**Comments:** The existing inline thread at line 92 remains relevant for this close-path gap, so no duplicate inline comment was posted.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview hardening remains optional</summary>

**Cause:** Deploy and teardown POSTs have timeouts but no bounded retry behavior, and undocumented `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY` values are not rejected or warned on.

**Candidate solutions:** Add safe bounded retries where the server operation is idempotent, and explicitly validate policy values or document the fallback. Updating closed preview comments to indicate teardown is also a deferrable UX improvement.

**Comments:** These are INFO-level polish items and should not drive automation unless the branch is already being touched.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: the remaining blocker and teardown warning are concrete workflow changes and are suitable for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Move deploy/comment/teardown into trusted default-branch workflow code so PR-authored workflow shell cannot run with OIDC or PR-write permissions.
- [ ] Fix teardown so a previously deployed same-repo human preview is torn down on PR close even if `sepo-preview` was removed before closing.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Add per-branch preview deployments via the Sepo preview server (OIDC, no secrets)
